required_validation_commands: pnpm.cmd --filter @matchpulse/api exec tsx --test src/competition-model-profile.test.ts src/prediction-engine-v1.test.ts src/odds-intelligence-public-mapper.test.ts src/competition-prediction-public-mapper.test.ts src/server-competition-prediction-route.test.ts src/competition-replay-fixtures.test.ts src/server-competition-replay-route.test.ts ../web/lib/competition-api.test.ts ../web/components/competition/CompetitionPredictionPanel.test.tsx
expected_results: the evaluator entry is available at /competition without wallet, payment, private token, or provider credential
live or stored public prediction mode consumes the existing versioned public-safe competition prediction API
deterministic replay fallback contains ordered opening, pressure-shift, and terminal checkpoints
replay visibly changes score, phase, market reliability, freshness, agreement, volatility, confidence, and risk
every required prediction family renders
mandatory human-readable market analysis renders availability, reliability, freshness, coverage, agreement, volatility, counts, movements, summary, limitations, and last update
prediction and market analysis remain visually and semantically distinct
loading, unavailable, no-data, replay-fallback, stale, partial, and terminal states remain bounded and explicit
public API responses and browser props exclude provider identities, raw payloads, assessment IDs, feature references, formulas, thresholds, weights, component scores, credentials, and proof blobs
no prescriptive wagering, stake, payout, profit, expected-value, wallet, payment, or financial recommendation feature is introduced
API focused tests pass
web client and component focused tests pass
API typecheck and production build pass
full API regression passes
web typecheck and production build pass
Prisma and migration diff is empty
no real application network access occurs during validation
no database mutation or migration is applied
no unrelated frontend file is changed
no successor phase is activated
